preamble: Diphthongization of the nasalized high vowel ĩ into the rising diphthong ie, often involving Lowering of the first element.

examples.note: Breaking of nasal high vowels during the transition to Old French.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
